elements.sent-2411-001: Recent results on the identified charged-hadron ($\pi^\pm$, $K^\pm$, $p$, $\bar{p}$) production at midrapidity region ($|\eta|<$ 0.35) have been measured by the PHENIX experiment in \pAl, \HeAu, \CuAu collisions at \snn~=~200~GeV and \UU collisions at \snn~=~193~GeV.
elements.sent-2411-002: These measurements are presented through the invariant transverse-momentum ($p_T$) and transverse-mass ($m_T$) spectra for different collision centralities.
elements.sent-2411-003: The averaged freeze-out temperature value for different systems was found to be $166.1 \pm 2.2$ MeV , and do not exhibit any dependence on the collision centrality and \Npart values.
elements.sent-2411-004: The particle ratios of $K/\pi$ and $p/\pi$ have been measured in different centrality ranges of large and small collision systems.
elements.sent-2411-005: The values of $K/\pi$ ratios measured in all considered collision systems were found to be consistent with those measured in $p\begin{equation}+\end{equation}p$ collisions.
elements.sent-2411-006: Furthermore, the identified charged-hadron nuclear-modification factors ($R_{AB}$) are also presented.
elements.sent-2411-007: Enhancement of proton $R_{AB}$ values over meson $R_{AB}$ values was observed in central $^3$He$+$Au, Cu$+$Au, and U$+$U collisions.
elements.sent-2411-008: The proton $R_{AB}$ values measured in $p\begin{equation}+$Al collision system were found to be consistent with $R_{AB}$ values of $\phi$, $\pi^\pm$, $K^\pm$, and $\pi^0$ mesons, suggesting that the size of the system produced in $p\end{equation}+$Al collisions is too small for recombination to cause a noticeable increase in proton production.
elements.formula-2411-002: \begin{equation}+$Al collision system were found to be consistent with $R_{AB}$ values of $\phi$, $\pi^\pm$, $K^\pm$, and $\pi^0$ mesons, suggesting that the size of the system produced in $p\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-009: Light hadrons are considerably produced in high-energy heavy-ion collisions and provide a wealth of information about properties of created QCD medium and reaction dynamics.
elements.sent-2411-010: These include, in particular, the implications of collective flow in small and large systems and the impact of recombination on baryon and strangeness enhancement.
elements.sent-2411-011: The system size dependence studies of different observable are crucial to investigate the properties of quark-gluon plasma (QGP) and hadronization based on initial conditions of the collisions.
elements.sent-2411-012: This paper presents recently finalized measurements by PHENIX on the production of identified charged hadron ($\pi^\pm$, $K^\pm$, $p$, $\bar{p}$) in small and large system size, $p$+Al, $p$/d/${^3}$He/Cu+Au collisions at \snn = 200 GeV, and U+U collisions at \snn = 193 GeV, and results were published recently~\cite{ppg249 .}
elements.sent-2411-013: The data sets used in the present analysis were collected by the PHENIX experiment~\cite{PHENIXoverview .}
elements.sent-2411-014: The latter consisted of three components: global detectors, the central spectrometers, and the muon arm spectrometers.
elements.sent-2411-015: These measurements use the central spectrometers ($|\eta|<0.35$), and provide precise tracking and particle identification for electrons, charged hadrons, and photons.
elements.sent-2411-016: Tracking and momentum determination are provided by drift chambers and pad chambers.
elements.sent-2411-017: Particle identification is provided by time of flight, ring-imaging Cherenkov detectors, and electromagnetic calorimeters.
elements.section-2411-002: Identified Charged Hadron Results in Small and Large Systems\label{sec-2}
elements.sent-2411-018: We observe that the $\pi$, $K$, and $p$ invariant spectra reveal different shapes as a function of \pt.
elements.sent-2411-019: To assess these differences, invariant-transverse-mass ($m_T=\sqrt{p_T^2 + m_0^2}$) spectra were calculated.
elements.sent-2411-020: We observe that the \mt invariant spectra of all identified charged hadrons have exponential form for \mt $<$ 1.5~GeV and was approximated using following fit function: \begin{equation}\label{eq:spectra_mt} \frac{1}{2\pi m_T} \frac{d^2 N}{dm_T dy}= \frac{A }{2\pi T (T+m_0)}\exp \left( -\frac{m_T - m_0}{T}\right)\end{equation} where $T$ is the inverse-slope parameter, $A$ is a normalization factor, and ${m_0}$ mass of the charged particle at rest.
elements.sent-2411-021: Figure~\ref{fig:Tinv shows examples of $T$ parameter vs.~hadron mass ($m_0$) dependencies for different centralities of \CuAu collisions.}
elements.sent-2411-022: Ordering of pion, kaon, and proton inverse slope values $T_{\pi <T_{K <T_p$ can be seen in all centralities.}}
elements.sent-2411-023: By doing similar work in all systems, \pal, \heau, and \UU, we obtained that the $T$ values, calculated for pions in different centralities, are nearly of the same values in all collision systems.
elements.sent-2411-024: The $T$ values, calculated for kaons, take intermediate values between pion and proton $T$-parameter values.
elements.sent-2411-025: The dotted lines on Fig.~\ref{fig:Tinv represent linear fits of the $T(m_0)$ values from each centrality bin using thermal formula $ T = T_0 +m \left< u_t\right>^2,$ where $T_0$ can be interpreted as a freeze-out temperature and \ut as the average collective velocity for all particle species.}
elements.sent-2411-026: The fit parameters for positively charged ($T_{0 ^{+}$, $\left<u_{t}\right>^{+}$) and negatively charged ($T_{0 ^{-}$, $\left<u_{t}\right>^{-}$) hadrons calculated in \pal, \heau, Cu$+$Au, and U$+$U collision systems are presented in Fig.~\ref{fig:T0Npart as a function of \Npart values.}}}
elements.sent-2411-027: The $T_0$ values calculated in collisions with different geometries and centralities were found to be coincident within uncertainties, indicating that the freeze-out temperature is approximately independent of \Npart values.
elements.sent-2411-028: The averaged $T_0$ value was found to be \mbox{$166.1 \pm 2.2$ MeV and is shown in the Fig.~\ref{fig:T0Npart with red solid line.}}
elements.sent-2411-029: Based on this consideration, the ratios of \prot/\pip, \aprot/\pim in different centralities of large (\cuau, \uu) and small (\pp, \pal, \heau) collision systems have been measured and published recently by the PHENIX collaboration~\cite{ppg256 .}
elements.sent-2411-030: We observed in central collisions of large systems the $p$/$\pi$ ratios reach the values of $\approx$ 0.6, but in peripheral collisions the values of $p/\pi$ ratios are smaller than 0.4 in the whole \pt range.
elements.sent-2411-031: Furthermore, in small collision systems (\pal, \heau), the values of $p$/$\pi$ ratios are in good agreement to those measured in \pp collisions~\cite{CH_pp .}
elements.sent-2411-032: The \rab values are found similar for collision systems with different geometries, but with the same \Npart values, indicating that identified charged-hadron production depends only on system size and not geometry.
elements.sent-2411-033: In addition, we observe the following features: 1) the slope of $R_{AB (p_T)$ in \pal collisions is flatter than it is in \heau and \dAu collisions, and 2) proton \rab values in \pal collisions at the intermediate \pt range (1.0~GeV/$c$~$<~p_T~<$~2.5 GeV/$c$) are equal to unity, while in \heau and \dAu collisions proton \rab is above unity.}
elements.sent-2411-034: These observations suggest that the differences between \pal and $d/$\heau might be caused by the size of the \pal system being insufficient to observe an increase in proton production..
elements.section-2411-003: Comparison of Identified Charged Hadron with ${ \phi}
elements.sent-2411-035: All these measurements of \rab were obtained by the PHENIX experiment~\cite{ppg256,phi .}
elements.sent-2411-036: These results elucidate following features: 1) in large collision systems and in the $^{3}$He+Au collision system, proton \rab values are enhanced over all meson \rab values.
elements.sent-2411-037: Knowing that the mass of the $\phi$-meson \mbox{$m_{\phi}$=1019 MeV/$c^2$} is similar to the proton mass \mbox{$m_{p}$=938 MeV/$c^2$}, therefore the enhancement of proton $R_{AB}$ values over $\phi$-meson $R_{AB}$ values suggests differences in baryon versus meson production instead of a simple mass dependence, 2) in \pal collisions proton $R_{AB}$ values and $R_{AB}$ values of all measured mesons are in good agreement within uncertainties.
elements.sent-2411-038: The PHENIX experiment has established a comprehensive program to study the light hadron ($\phi$, \pipm, \Kpm, \prots and $\pi^0$) production in small and large collisions systems by measuring invariant transverse-momentum and transverse-mass spectra, particle ratios, and nuclear modification factors.
elements.sent-2411-039: These results were presented and discussed in the scope to study the properties of created QCD medium and reaction dynamics at RHIC energies.
